chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Hello all, welcome to the video lecture series of optical communication.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Subject code is REC 075 and here we are in lecture number 4.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So what we have covered in the previous lecture, we talked about optical waveguide, what is

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: the structure of your optical fiber like core, cladding, what is core, what is cladding and

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: what is the reflective index.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: The next one we talked about is very important, it is called ray transmission theory.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: How light propagates in optical fiber.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: There is incident ray, reflected ray, angle of incidence, angle of reflection, Snell's

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: law, all the things.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then we talked about TIR, that is the condition for light propagation in fiber.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: That is called TIR, total internal reflection.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: From there we showed what is critical angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: As I said in the last class, these are the parameters related to your light theory.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: In this lecture, we are going to talk about other parameters which are related to optical

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: fiber light theory.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: One is called NA, that is called Numerical Aperture and one is called the Acceptance

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay, so let's start.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: First, we will talk about Numerical Aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: But before that, if I talk about this topic, this is called Meri-Daniel Rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What is Meri-Daniel Rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: When we propagate our ray in optical fiber, means when the light ray is propagated inside

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: optical fiber, then based on the propagation, your ray is classified into two types.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: One is called Meri-Daniel and one is called Skew Rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What are Skew Rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We will talk about Skew Rays in the next lecture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We are not talking about Skew Rays now.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now we will talk about Meri-Daniel Rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What are Meri-Daniel Rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: The rays which passes through the axis of the fiber core are called Meri-Daniel Rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: The rays which pass through the optical fiber core are called Meri-Daniel Rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, these rays are also divided into two categories.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Some rays are like this.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If I talk about this diagram, this is my optical fiber which we have shown here.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this is your fiber axis.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, once the ray is coming into this fiber,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this ray will propagate up to this point and after this point and then goes back into the air.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is called Unbound Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Because the rays which are not bounded inside the optical fiber will go out.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: The rays which are not bounded inside the optical fiber will go out.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: But, the rays which are not propagated inside the optical fiber are called Unbound Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If you look at the other ray,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: suppose this ray comes here and this ray reaches here.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is inside of the core.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then, your reflection will go back to the core end.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then, it will reach here and reflect here and then it will propagate again.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then, it will move inside the fiber.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this type of ray is called Bound Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, let's talk about Meri-Daniel Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: The rays which pass through the fiber axis are called Meri-Daniel Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: But, Meri-Daniel Ray is divided into two categories.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: One is called Bound Ray and the other is called Unbound Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What are the Bound Rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: These rays are trapped in the core and propagate along the fiber axis.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Like this.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And what are the Unbound Rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: These rays are reflected out of the fiber core.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: These rays are reflected out of the fiber core and back into the air or in the cladding itself.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is called Unbound Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is called Meri-Daniel Ray.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What do you mean by rays?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If the rays in the fiber are like Hormones, then you can explain it.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Next, come to the main topic that is called the Numerical Aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Numerical Aperture is a mathematical term related to this fiber theory.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, let's see what it is.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: It is a proof and it has numerical questions which we will see in the next lecture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, let's see Numerical Aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: To define Numerical Aperture, I have used the same diagram which we discussed in the previous lecture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: That is my optical fiber part.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This portion is our core represented by N1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this portion is our cladding.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is the refractive index of N2.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, here it is N1 and N2.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, what will happen here?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: When the optical ray enters,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, here from here,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: the refractive index is N0.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: From here, the ray will enter.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is basically the medium interface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If you see,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this is basically the medium interface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is air and this is basically the core.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Between the air and the core, the ray will enter.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is my interface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this axis works as a normal to the interface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: As we have studied earlier,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: when a ray is incident,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this is the B-ray which is incident.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: When B-ray is incident,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: the angle formed with the normal is called the angle of incident.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is angle of incidence.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Similarly, this ray is refracted in the medium.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this angle again made with the normal to the surface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is called the angle of refraction.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Angle of refraction.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Here, we assume that no ray has returned.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Because this is the air and cladding part.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, we assume that the incident ray will come with an incident angle theta A

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: and refracted or we can say transmitted into our fiber core

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: with an angle alpha M.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Okay.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, as per this Nernst law,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: if the incident ray is this with an angle theta A

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: and refractive index is N0,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: what we can write?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: N0 sin theta A is equal to

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this is N1 sin alpha M.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, N0 sin theta is equal to N1 sin alpha M.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, if you see in this diagram,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: when the ray enters the core,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this filter,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: at this point,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: the ray will hit the body of the core.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, as soon as there is an incident or strike,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: now this becomes the incident ray

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: because this is medium 1,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: and this is medium 2 and 2,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: and this is normal to the surface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is normal to both medium.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this is the surface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is the angle of incidence,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: normal to the surface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, where do we have to follow this?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: TIR,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Total Internal Reflection.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, here it is TIR condition.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, for TIR condition,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: basically, phi angle of incidence will be equal to phi c.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If I say phi c in the diagram,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: it will be easier to see that this is a light ray

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: which is following the total internal reflection

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: and it will totally internal reflect.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, from this diagram,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: if we see that this angle is alpha m,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this angle is phi c,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this angle is 90 degrees,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this is normal to the surface.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, what can I write in the value of alpha m?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: I can write 90 degrees minus phi c.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, in this equation, here it is alpha m.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, alpha m is replaced by pi by 2 minus phi c.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And as soon as you replace alpha m by pi by 2 minus phi c,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: then sin pi by 2 minus theta,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: that is, sin 90 minus theta,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: this is cos theta.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this will be written as n1 cos of phi c.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, n0 sin theta A is equal to n1 cos of phi c.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, if I talk about n0,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: first, calculate the value of sin theta A.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, sin theta A is equal to n1 by n0 under root of 1 minus sin square theta d.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Because cos square theta plus sin square theta is equal to 1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, what is the value of cos theta?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Cos theta is equal to n dot of 1 minus sin square theta.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, using this value, we have converted it.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, as we read in the last lecture, critical angle value.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, the value of critical angle is sin of phi c is equal to n2 by n1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, according to this formula,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: you can replace the value of sin square phi c here.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: That will be replaced by n2 square by n1 square.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Also, n0 is over here because n0 is basically air.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Air is medium. So, n0 is equal to 1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, n0 is equal to 1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, we will get the formula of sin theta A.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: That is equal to n1 under root of 1 minus n2 whole square divided by n1 whole square.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If we take the LCM,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: it will be n1 square minus n2 square divided by n1 square whole under root.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This term will cancel out n1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, the remaining term is n1 square minus n2 square.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This particular term is called numerical aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is how numerical aperture is derived.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, you have a question based on this where you are given the value of n1 and n2.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We can directly calculate the value of numerical aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Next, we can derive numerical aperture from another formula.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: That is also defined as in terms of relative refractive index difference, del.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Where del is defined as n1 square minus n2 square divided by twice of n1 square.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This can approximately be written as n1 minus n2 divided by n1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is the refractive index formula for del is much less than 1.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: But, I mainly have a requirement for this formula.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, if we look at the back, we derived numerical aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is n1 square minus n2 square whole under root.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, if I want to subtract this under root, what will happen here?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: na square is equal to n1 square minus n2 square.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, if we look at the value of n1 square minus n2 square from here,

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: what will be the value from here?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This value is written as twice n1 square into del.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, if I want the value of na, what will ultimately be the value of na?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If we square this n1, n1 will come out and this will be the note of 2 del.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, this is the expression for numerical aperture in terms of relative refractive index del.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: When you are given del, you have to calculate numerical aperture with the help of this formula.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Else, we can directly use this formula n1 square minus n2 square under root.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Got it? So, what is numerical aperture?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We understood it. We also saw the expression.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then, how we can calculate another formula for the numerical aperture?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: This is the formula related to relative refractive index difference.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Then, the last one is called Perceptive Angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Now, what is Perceptive Angle? Another numerical term.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If I talk about this diagram, then this angle in this diagram, this value is theta A.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, minimum value of this angle, on which the ray can propagate inside the fiber core.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Inside the fiber core, that particular angle is called angle of acceptance.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Here, simply called acceptance angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, theta A is the maximum acceptance angle to the axis at which light may enter the fiber in order to be propagated.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: And this often referred as acceptance angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Got it? So, after learning this, now we can easily say what is numerical aperture?

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We derived the formula for numerical aperture.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: If you are asked the question of numerical aperture, you can derive it.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: What do you mean by acceptance angle? We have done that also.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, now in the next lecture, we will solve numerical problems on these two topics.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: We will solve questions on numerical aperture, acceptance angle, and critical angle.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Along with that, we will talk about skew rays.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: Till then, you can watch this proof again.

chunks.title: Numerical Aperture and Acceptance Angle
chunks.text: So, keep practicing.

text: Hello all, welcome to the video lecture series of optical communication. Subject code is REC 075 and here we are in lecture number 4. So what we have covered in the previous lecture, we talked about optical waveguide, what is the structure of your optical fiber like core, cladding, what is core, what is cladding and what is the reflective index. The next one we talked about is very important, it is called ray transmission theory. How light propagates in optical fiber. There is incident ray, reflected ray, angle of incidence, angle of reflection, Snell's law, all the things. Then we talked about TIR, that is the condition for light propagation in fiber. That is called TIR, total internal reflection. From there we showed what is critical angle. As I said in the last class, these are the parameters related to your light theory. In this lecture, we are going to talk about other parameters which are related to optical fiber light theory. One is called NA, that is called Numerical Aperture and one is called the Acceptance Angle. Okay, so let's start. First, we will talk about Numerical Aperture. But before that, if I talk about this topic, this is called Meri-Daniel Rays. What is Meri-Daniel Rays? When we propagate our ray in optical fiber, means when the light ray is propagated inside optical fiber, then based on the propagation, your ray is classified into two types. One is called Meri-Daniel and one is called Skew Rays. What are Skew Rays? We will talk about Skew Rays in the next lecture. We are not talking about Skew Rays now. Now we will talk about Meri-Daniel Rays. What are Meri-Daniel Rays? The rays which passes through the axis of the fiber core are called Meri-Daniel Rays. The rays which pass through the optical fiber core are called Meri-Daniel Rays. Now, these rays are also divided into two categories. Some rays are like this. If I talk about this diagram, this is my optical fiber which we have shown here. And this is your fiber axis.
